seoTitle: Peak Vale vs Peel Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Peak Vale and Peel Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Peak Vale vs Peel Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Peak Vale and Peel Island.
heading: Peak Vale vs Peel Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Peak Vale, Queensland and Peel Island, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Peel Island has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Peak Vale (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Peak Vale or Peel Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Peak Vale has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Peel Island scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Peak Vale scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Peel Island scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Peak Vale or Peel Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Peel Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
